title: Phrasal Verbs Set 1
description: Learn 30 essential phrasal verbs through a startup story. Understand separable vs inseparable phrasal verbs.
descriptionTr: Bir startup hikayesi üzerinden 30 temel öbek fiili öğren. Ayrılabilir ve ayrılamaz öbek fiilleri anla.

vocabulary.example: I want to set up my own business next year.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gelecek yıl kendi işimi kurmak istiyorum.
vocabulary.extra: Can you help me set up this computer?
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu bilgisayarı kurmama yardım eder misin?

vocabulary.example: Don't give up! Your idea is great.
vocabulary.extra: He gave up smoking last month.

vocabulary.example: I picked up some new skills at work.
vocabulary.extra: Can you pick up the package from the office?

vocabulary.example: Turn on the laptop and check your email.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Laptopı aç ve e-postanı kontrol et.
vocabulary.extra: She turned on the lights in the meeting room.

vocabulary.example: Please turn off your phone during the meeting.
vocabulary.extra: I always turn off the computer before I leave.
vocabulary.extraTr: Çıkmadan önce bilgisayarı her zaman kapatırım.

vocabulary.example: We are looking for a new investor.
vocabulary.extra: What kind of job are you looking for?

vocabulary.example: I looked up the company online before the meeting.
vocabulary.extra: If you don't know a word, look it up in the dictionary.

vocabulary.example: I found out that our competitor has a new product.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Rakibimizin yeni bir ürünü olduğunu öğrendim.
vocabulary.extra: How did you find out about this event?

vocabulary.example: We need to figure out a better marketing strategy.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Daha iyi bir pazarlama stratejisi bulmamız lazım.
vocabulary.extra: I can't figure out how to use this software.

vocabulary.example: I hope the deal works out for both sides.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Umarım anlaşma iki taraf için de iyi sonuçlanır.
vocabulary.extra: We worked out the problems with the website.

vocabulary.example: She came up with an amazing idea for the app.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uygulama için harika bir fikir buldu.
vocabulary.extra: We need to come up with a name for the company.
vocabulary.extraTr: Şirket için bir isim bulmamız lazım.

vocabulary.example: We are running out of time before the deadline.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Son teslim tarihinden önce zamanımız tükeniyor.
vocabulary.extra: The startup ran out of money after six months.
vocabulary.extraTr: Startup altı ay sonra parasını tüketti.

vocabulary.example: I get along with my business partner very well.
vocabulary.extra: Do you get along with your colleagues?

vocabulary.example: It's hard to keep up with new technology.
vocabulary.extra: I can't keep up with all these emails!

vocabulary.example: Don't put off your business plan. Start today!
vocabulary.extra: We put off the meeting until next week.

vocabulary.example: She brought up a good point at the meeting.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Toplantıda güzel bir noktaya dikkat çekti.
vocabulary.extra: I don't want to bring up this topic again.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu konuyu tekrar gündeme getirmek istemiyorum.

vocabulary.example: Even when things were difficult, we carried on.
vocabulary.exampleTr: İşler zor olduğunda bile devam ettik.
vocabulary.extra: Carry on with your work, I'll wait.

vocabulary.example: Our delivery truck broke down on the highway.
vocabulary.extra: Let me break down the costs for you.

vocabulary.example: A bigger company wants to take over our startup.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Daha büyük bir şirket startup'ımızı devralmak istiyor.
vocabulary.extra: Can you take over this project while I'm away?
vocabulary.extraTr: Ben yokken bu projeyi devralabilir misin?

vocabulary.example: Please fill in this registration form.
vocabulary.extra: Can you fill me in on what happened at the meeting?
vocabulary.extraTr: Toplantıda ne olduğu hakkında beni bilgilendirir misin?

vocabulary.example: Over 500 users signed up in the first week.
vocabulary.exampleTr: İlk haftada 500'den fazla kullanıcı kayıt oldu.
vocabulary.extra: You should sign up for this free online course.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu ücretsiz online kursa kayıt olmalısın.

vocabulary.example: Log in to your account to see your dashboard.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Kontrol panelinizi görmek için hesabınıza giriş yapın.
vocabulary.extra: I can't log in. I forgot my password.

vocabulary.example: Check out our new website! It looks amazing.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni web sitemize bir bak! Harika görünüyor.
vocabulary.extra: I checked out the competition and we are ahead.

vocabulary.example: He dropped out of college to start his company.
vocabulary.extra: Many famous entrepreneurs dropped out of school.

vocabulary.example: Only three investors showed up at our presentation.
vocabulary.extra: He always shows up late to meetings.

vocabulary.example: Your product needs to stand out from the competition.
vocabulary.extra: What makes your startup stand out?

vocabulary.example: I ended up working 16 hours a day in the first year.
vocabulary.exampleTr: İlk yılda günde 16 saat çalışır hale geldim.
vocabulary.extra: We ended up choosing a different supplier.

vocabulary.example: We went through many difficulties in the beginning.
vocabulary.extra: Let's go through the business plan together.

vocabulary.example: We need to cut down our expenses this quarter.
vocabulary.extra: I'm trying to cut down on coffee. I drink too much.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kahveyi azaltmaya çalışıyorum. Çok fazla içiyorum.

vocabulary.example: Don't make up excuses. Just do the work!
vocabulary.extra: Women make up 60% of our customers.

grammar.title: Phrasal Verbs — Separable vs Inseparable
grammar.explanation: Phrasal verbs are verbs with a particle (up, down, on, off, out, in, etc.). Some phrasal verbs are SEPARABLE — the object can go between the verb and the particle. Some are INSEPARABLE — the object must go after the particle. With pronouns (him, it, them), separable phrasal verbs MUST be separated.
grammar.explanationTr: Öbek fiiller, bir fiil ve bir edattan (up, down, on, off, out, in vb.) oluşur. Bazı öbek fiiller AYRILABİLİR — nesne fiil ile edat arasına girebilir. Bazıları AYRILAMAZ — nesne mutlaka edattan sonra gelmelidir. Zamirlerle (him, it, them) kullanıldığında, ayrılabilir öbek fiiller MUTLAKA ayrılmalıdır.
table: Turn on the light. = Turn the light on. = Turn IT on.
turn on, turn off, pick up, set up, fill in, bring up, put off, look up, figure out, cut down, make up
Look for a job. = Look for IT. (NOT: Look IT for ✗)
look for, get along with, keep up with, come up with, run out of, go through, end up, show up, stand out, drop out
Turn it on. (NOT: Turn on it ✗)
Pick it up. / Fill it in. / Turn them off.
Come up with an idea. Run out of money.
come up with, get along with, keep up with, run out of

tips.en: Turn on the TV. = Turn the TV on. = Turn IT on.
tips.tr: Ayrılabilir: Her 3 form da doğru, ama zamirle MUTLAKA ayır

tips.en: Look for a job. (NOT: Look a job for ✗)
tips.tr: Ayrılamaz: Nesne her zaman edattan SONRA gelir

tips.en: I figured out the problem. = I figured the problem out. = I figured IT out.
tips.tr: Separable: 'figure out' ayrılabilir bir phrasal verb

tips.en: She came up with a great idea. (NOT: came a great idea up with ✗)
He ended up in Istanbul. / She showed up late.
The meaning often changes with different particles: look up ≠ look for ≠ look after
tips.tr: Farklı edatlarla anlam tamamen değişir: look up ≠ look for ≠ look after

reading.text:
My name is Deniz and I want to tell you my startup story. Two years ago, I came up with an idea for a food delivery app. Everyone told me to give up, but I didn't listen.

First, I had to figure out how to build the app. I signed up for an online coding course and picked up the basics in three months. Then I set up a small office in my apartment and started working.

In the beginning, everything went wrong. My laptop broke down twice. I ran out of money after four months. But I carried on because I believed in my idea.

I looked for investors everywhere. Only two showed up at my presentation, but one of them loved my app. He brought up some problems with the design, and I worked them out in one week.

Six months later, 10,000 users signed up for the app. I couldn't keep up with all the orders! I had to take over the customer service myself because we didn't have enough staff.

What made us stand out was our fast delivery. We never put off a delivery. If something went wrong, we found out immediately and fixed it.

I ended up working 80 hours a week, but it was worth it. My advice? Don't give up on your dreams. Check out your competition, cut down unnecessary costs, and come up with something that stands out. The journey is hard, but if you carry on, things will work out!
reading.textTr:
Benim adım Deniz ve size startup hikayemi anlatmak istiyorum. İki yıl önce, bir yemek teslimat uygulaması fikri buldum. Herkes bana vazgeçmemi söyledi ama dinlemedim.

Önce uygulamayı nasıl yapacağımı çözmem gerekiyordu. Bir online kodlama kursuna kayıt oldum ve üç ayda temel bilgileri öğrendim. Sonra dairemdeki küçük bir ofis kurdum ve çalışmaya başladım.

Başlangıçta her şey ters gitti. Laptopum iki kez bozuldu. Dört ay sonra param bitti. Ama fikrimin gücüne inandığım için devam ettim.

Her yerde yatırımcı aradım. Sunumuma sadece ikisi geldi ama biri uygulamamı çok beğendi. Tasarımla ilgili bazı sorunları gündeme getirdi ve ben bunları bir haftada çözdüm.

Altı ay sonra 10.000 kullanıcı uygulamaya kayıt oldu. Tüm siparişlere yetişemiyordum! Yeterli personelimiz olmadığı için müşteri hizmetlerini kendim devralmak zorunda kaldım.

Bizi öne çıkaran hızlı teslimatımızdı. Hiçbir teslimatı ertelemezdik. Bir şeyler ters giderse hemen öğrenirdik ve düzeltirdik.

Sonunda haftada 80 saat çalışır hale geldim ama buna değdi. Tavsiyem? Hayallerinizden vazgeçmeyin. Rekabetinizi inceleyin, gereksiz masrafları azaltın ve öne çıkan bir şey bulun. Yolculuk zor ama devam ederseniz işler yoluna girer!

questions.question: What kind of app did Deniz create?
questions.questionTr: Deniz ne tür bir uygulama yaptı?

questions.question: How did Deniz learn to code?
options: He signed up for an online course
He already knew how to code

questions.question: How many investors showed up at his presentation?
What made Deniz's app stand out?
What is Deniz's advice?
options: Give up if things are hard
Don't give up, check competition, cut costs, stand out

fillBlanks.sentence: I want to ___ ___ my own company.
Please ___ ___ your phone during the meeting.
We ___ ___ ___ coffee. Can you buy some?
I need to ___ ___ how this works.
She ___ ___ a very important issue at the meeting.
He ___ ___ of university to start a business.

multipleChoice.question: "Turn on the computer" — Zamirle nasıl söylenir?
"I'm looking for a job." — 'look for' ne tür bir phrasal verb?
"Sonunda İstanbul'da yaşar oldum." İngilizce'de hangisi?
options: I ended up living in Istanbul.

multipleChoice.question: "She came up with a great idea." Ne anlama gelir?
"Fill ___ this form, please." Boşluğa ne gelir?
"We ran out of time." Ne anlama gelir?
"Pick it up" — 'it' neden arada?
options: Çünkü 'pick up' ayrılabilir ve zamirle MUTLAKA ayrılır
Çünkü 'it' her zaman fiilden sonra gelir

sentenceOrder.correct: Don't give up on your dreams
I need to figure out this problem
She came up with a great idea
He signed up for the course
I ended up working very late
Can you fill in this form
Our product needs to stand out

translation.english: I want to set up my own business.
acceptAlso: I want to set my own business up
Do not give up! Carry on!

translation.english: I'm looking for a new job.
acceptAlso: I am looking for a new job

translation.english: I can't figure out how to solve this.
acceptAlso: I cannot figure out how to solve this
We have run out of money

translation.english: I ended up moving to Istanbul.
acceptAlso: I ended up moving to İstanbul

translation.english: We need to cut down our expenses.
acceptAlso: We need to cut down costs
We have to cut down our expenses

dialog.title: Talking to a Startup Founder
dialog.context: Eyüp bir iş etkinliğinde startup kurucusu Mert ile tanışıyor. Mert, online e-ticaret platformunun hikayesini anlatıyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Hi Mert! I heard you set up your own company. That's amazing!
lines.textTr: Selam Mert! Kendi şirketini kurduğunu duydum. Harika!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Thanks! Yeah, I set it up about two years ago. It was really hard in the beginning.
lines.textTr: Teşekkürler! Evet, yaklaşık iki yıl önce kurdum. Başlangıçta gerçekten zordu.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: How did you come up with the idea?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I was looking for a good e-commerce platform for small businesses and I couldn't find one. So I decided to build one myself.
lines.textTr: Küçük işletmeler için iyi bir e-ticaret platformu arıyordum ama bulamadım. Ben de kendim yapmaya karar verdim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Did you ever want to give up?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Many times! We ran out of money after six months. My laptop broke down. Some days, nobody showed up at the office.
lines.textTr: Defalarca! Altı ay sonra paramız tükendi. Laptopum bozuldu. Bazı günler ofise kimse gelmedi.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: But you carried on. What kept you going?
lines.textTr: Ama devam ettin. Seni devam ettiren ne oldu?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I found out that 500 people signed up for the beta version. That gave me energy to keep going.
lines.textTr: 500 kişinin beta versiyona kayıt olduğunu öğrendim. Bu bana devam etme enerjisi verdi.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That's great! How did you stand out from the competition?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: We figured out that small businesses need simple tools. We cut down all the unnecessary features and made it super easy.
lines.textTr: Küçük işletmelerin basit araçlara ihtiyacı olduğunu çözdük. Gereksiz özellikleri azalttık ve çok kolay hale getirdik.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Smart! I heard a big company wanted to take over your platform?
lines.textTr: Akıllıca! Büyük bir şirketin platformunuzu devralmak istediğini duydum?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Yes, they brought up a big offer, but I turned it down. I want to keep building.
lines.textTr: Evet, büyük bir teklif gündeme getirdiler ama reddettim. Kurmaya devam etmek istiyorum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I'm thinking about starting something too. I want to look into e-commerce logistics.
lines.textTr: Ben de bir şeyler başlamayı düşünüyorum. E-ticaret lojistiğine bakmak istiyorum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Go for it! Don't put it off. Look up the market, check out your competition, and just start.
lines.textTr: Hadi başla! Erteleme. Pazarı araştır, rekabeti incele ve başla.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thanks, Mert. I need to figure out a business plan first, but I won't give up!
lines.textTr: Teşekkürler, Mert. Önce bir iş planı çözmem lazım ama vazgeçmeyeceğim!

questions.question: When did Mert set up his company?
How many people signed up for the beta?
questions.questionTr: Beta versiyona kaç kişi kayıt oldu?

questions.question: Did Mert accept the big company's offer?
questions.questionTr: Mert büyük şirketin teklifini kabul etti mi?
options: He is still thinking about it

questions.question: What is Eyüp interested in starting?

resources.title: Phrasal Verbs for Business - BBC Learning English
Separable vs Inseparable Phrasal Verbs - British Council
resources.description: Ayrılabilir ve ayrılamaz phrasal verb'ler interaktif alıştırmalar
